scope: First 10 incomplete transfer rows by sourceRow
method: Research player role/minutes expectation from roster pages, school announcements, transfer reports, and credible team coverage.
Use historical rating from the imported spreadsheet as the historical anchor.
Translate evidence into the manual input fields used by the spreadsheet: starter, role, opportunity change, offensive burden, NBA projection, and upside/tools.
Keep every researched suggestion in review status until manually accepted.

teamContextRubric.projectedStarter: Use Yes only with projected-lineup evidence, explicit likely-starter reporting, or a roster context with a clear open starting path. Use Uncertain for high-minute players without clear starting evidence.
teamContextRubric.projectedRole: Cap likely rotation or specialist profiles in the 60-75 range. Use 80-89 for likely starters or top-four options. Use 90+ only for projected primary/near-primary stars.
teamContextRubric.opportunityChange: Do not reward prior production by itself. Lower-major stars moving into crowded high-major rosters should usually sit in the 45-65 range unless reporting shows a clear role increase.
teamContextRubric.offensiveBurden: Tie burden to expected usage within the new lineup, not just prior usage.
teamContextRubric.roleCap: Each researched player gets a role cap based on projected lineup, returning stars, incoming stars, position competition, and starter evidence.

players.researchSummary: Strong Weber State production and usage, but the sheet has no upcoming team. A July 2026 Gonzaga roster-needs article lists him as an available option, not as a committed player. Do not score final opportunity until destination is known.

teamContext.roleCap: hold until current team is known

evidence.notes: Started all 32 games in 2025-26, first-team All-Big Sky, 17.5 ppg, 4.3 apg, elite free throw shooting.
Listed among possible Gonzaga guard options as of mid-July 2026, not reported as committed.

players.researchSummary: Treat as a likely high-minute guard, but not an automatic starter until SMU lineup context is confirmed. SMU lists him on the 2026-27 roster after a Tulane season where he started every game, led the team across scoring/rebounds/assists/steals, and played 36.1 minutes. The ACC/high-major jump and lack of direct projected-lineup evidence cap starter/role/opportunity.

teamContext.positionCompetition: unknown guard competition at SMU; do not assume Tulane role transfers 1:1
teamContext.starterEvidence: production suggests major minutes, but no direct starter projection found in this batch
teamContext.roleCap: high-minute rotation/possible starter; cap role around 80 until lineup source supports more

evidence.notes: 2026-27 roster bio: started all 33 games at Tulane; 19.1 ppg, 4.9 rpg, 4.6 apg, 1.6 spg, 36.1 mpg.
Confirms SMU 2026-27 roster placement.

players.researchSummary: Strong fit/role signal, but revised down from primary-star treatment. George Mason announced his return and explicitly framed him as someone who knows Tony Skinn's system. He was First Team All-SoCon after ranking second in that league in scoring, so the projection gives him a starter/major-guard opportunity but not a 90+ role without a lineup projection.

teamContext.projectedLineup: not fully projected in sourced material
teamContext.returningStars: Tony Skinn system familiarity is positive, but team hierarchy still needs lineup pass
teamContext.incomingStars: George Mason transfer class context supports meaningful role
teamContext.positionCompetition: guard role likely strong because of production and prior George Mason connection
teamContext.starterEvidence: strong circumstantial evidence from return/system fit, not explicit projected lineup
teamContext.roleCap: likely starter/major-minute guard; cap role around 85

evidence.notes: Returned to George Mason; First Team All-SoCon at Mercer; 19.7 ppg; coach notes system familiarity.
Team preview language identifies Okojie in nationally ranked transfer class and projected Patriot core.

players.researchSummary: Strong scoring addition, but revised from likely-star to possible-starter/major-rotation. WVU added him after an All-Big East level scoring season at Butler, and WVU coverage describes him as a playmaker/scorer who could play guard or wing. The word 'could' matters: without a projected lineup, starter and opportunity change should be capped.

teamContext.positionCompetition: could play SG/wing, but role is not guaranteed from sources
teamContext.starterEvidence: coverage says could start/play guard or wing; not definitive starter language
teamContext.roleCap: possible starter/major rotation; cap role around 80

evidence.notes: Committed to WVU; All-Big East third team; 17.1 ppg; shooting profile; possible SG/wing usage.
Big East Player of the Week; second in Big East scoring at the time; high-volume scoring/shooting.
WVU coach quote describes him as playmaker who can score and get others involved.

players.researchSummary: Clear elite-shooter addition, revised slightly lower because Tennessee added a large transfer group and his likely usage is more specialist/spacing than primary creator. Give solid minutes, uncertain starter status, lower burden, and low NBA/upside scores despite high college value.

teamContext.returningStars: Tennessee rotation needs separate projected-lineup pass
teamContext.incomingStars: large transfer group creates role competition
teamContext.positionCompetition: elite shooting gives path to minutes, but crowded roster caps role
teamContext.starterEvidence: no direct starter projection found in this batch
teamContext.roleCap: specialist/rotation shooter; cap role in mid-60s unless lineup supports more

evidence.notes: MVC Player of the Year; described by Rick Barnes as one of the best shooters in college basketball; career 43.5% from three.
First portal commitment; 219 threes over two seasons at Belmont; 43.9% from three.
Tennessee added eight transfers, creating role competition.

players.researchSummary: Strongest role/talent signal in this batch, but trimmed slightly to account for other projected Kentucky stars. Kentucky signed him as a major portal addition at point guard after he started 29 games at Washington. Current coverage describes him settling in as Kentucky's starting point guard, so role, opportunity, and burden remain high.

teamContext.projectedLineup: source reports him stepping in as Kentucky's point guard
teamContext.returningStars: Kentucky has other high-usage/high-talent pieces, so burden is high but not unchecked
teamContext.incomingStars: Kentucky roster includes multiple projected impact players
teamContext.positionCompetition: strongest direct PG role evidence in batch
teamContext.starterEvidence: direct report of starting point guard role
teamContext.roleCap: likely starter/lead guard; role can reach 90 but not 95+ unless clear primary-star usage emerges

evidence.notes: Signed with Kentucky; 29 starts at Washington; 15.7 ppg, 4.5 apg, 48.9% FG.
2026-27 Kentucky roster; confirms junior guard, previous school Washington, production profile.
First Kentucky portal commitment and described as a major addition at point guard.
Reports Diallo stepping in as Kentucky's starting point guard.

players.researchSummary: Top-end researched projection, but revised down from 90+ role because Illinois has other important pieces and the exact lineup hierarchy still needs confirmation. ESPN called him a top-10 transfer, and Illinois describes him as one of the best offensive players in the portal. He helps replace Illinois' starting backcourt after a Final Four season, with size, shooting, and playmaking upside.

teamContext.projectedLineup: not fully confirmed in sourced material, but roster construction suggests a major role
teamContext.returningStars: Illinois has other high-level pieces, limiting primary-star assumption
teamContext.incomingStars: Vaaks is a premier transfer, but not the only projected impact player
teamContext.positionCompetition: strong path to start/major role replacing backcourt losses
teamContext.starterEvidence: high-end transfer reporting and roster need support starter assumption, but no exact projected lineup cited
teamContext.roleCap: likely starter/top option; cap role around 85 until projected lineup confirms primary usage

evidence.notes: Big East All-Freshman, 15.8 ppg, 91 threes, 3.2 apg; Underwood calls him one of the best offensive players in the portal.
ESPN ranked him as a top-10 transfer; notes size, playmaking, offensive versatility, and role replacing Illinois backcourt losses.
Illinois top target and first portal pickup; focal point of Providence offense.
